name: Salvatore Greco
description: Sicilian Mafia boss, head of the Greci clan. Aged 55. Dedicated his life to resurrecting his family's influence after his father was murdered by the rival Matteo family in the 1990s. Has hunted down and killed all surviving Mattei. Personally responsible for at least 60 murders. Controls the Palermo drug trade, with enterprises worth an estimated $20-30 billion. Austere in tastes and pathologically averse to public attention.

name: Villanelle
aliases: ['Oxana' 'Oxana Vorontsova' 'Sylviane Morel' 'Irina Skoryk' 'Manon Lefebvre' 'Julia Fanin' 'Sylvie Dazat' 'Marina Falieri' 'Violette Duroc' 'Maria Bogomolova' 'Malgorzata Spurrell' 'Goz']
description: Oxana Borisovna Vorontsova, from Perm, around 25-26 years old. Professional assassin for the Twelve, codenamed Villanelle. Trained in Europe. Blonde, with grey eyes and a scar on her upper lip; she often wears her hair up. Slender, with toned arms. She is polyglot with an academic background in Linguistics, fluent in Russian, English, French and Italian, and delivers cover stories flawlessly. She has photographic memory and expertise in fashion and haute cuisine. Her demeanour alternates between cold detachment and playful seductiveness. In social settings she is sophisticated and charming; in professional contexts, blunt and direct.

name: Olivier
description: Young man in his mid-twenties, encountered at Le Jasmin club in Paris. Recently launched a career as an art dealer. Elegantly dishevelled with a languid posture and wry smile. Wry, confident, and flirtatious in manner, with a sophisticated upper-class French register.

name: Nica
description: Young woman in her mid-twenties, encountered at Le Jasmin club in Paris. Works intermittently as an actress. Cat-like and exquisite, with golden eyes and a cool, assessing manner. Direct and challenging in speech, favouring short declarative sentences, with a sophisticated upper-class French register.

name: Konstantin
description: Villanelle's former handler for the Twelve. Ex-head of operations at Directorate S, the espionage and agent-running wing of the SVR. His Operations Department was the Russian equivalent of the British E Squadron — special forces for deniable and deep-cover operations. Left the SVR ten years before the events of book 2. Calm, methodical, and authoritative in demeanour. Recruited Villanelle three years before the story begins.

name: Boris Vorontsov
description: Father of Villanelle/Oxana. Senior sergeant in the Russian Spetsnaz and close-combat instructor. Decorated veteran of Grozny and Pervomayskoye during the Chechen wars. Killed by the Brothers' Circle after falling out with gangsters he was moonlighting for.

name: Anna Leonova
description: French teacher in Perm who took a special interest in her troubled but linguistically gifted pupil Oxana Vorontsova. About forty, with soft tired eyes, a beret, and a faint smell of cigarettes. Gentle, patient, and principled. Contacted by an unknown source to help Eve in Perm after the Bolshoi events.

name: Calogera Greco
description: Wife of Salvatore Greco. Rarely leaves the fortified farmhouse outside Palermo.

name: Valentina Greco
description: Daughter of Salvatore Greco. Lives in a neighbouring village, married to the eldest son of her father's consigliere.

name: Bruno De Santis
description: Journalist at Corriere della Sera. Wrote an article criticising Franca Farfaglia for accepting a donation from Salvatore Greco. Survived a near-fatal hit-and-run in Trastevere, Rome.

name: Franca Farfaglia
description: Young Sicilian soprano, twenty-six years old. Studies at La Scala Theatre Academy in Milan. Commanding in appearance with proud, severe features. Accepted a donation from Salvatore Greco towards her studies.

name: Frank
description: Former Special Boat Service instructor, about sixty years old. Knotty and taciturn, with a gaze as cold as the North Sea. Wears a faded cotton tracksuit and old tennis shoes in all weathers. Trains Oxana in fitness and unarmed combat on the Essex coast. Teaches by physical example, having refined elements of ju-jitsu, street-fighting, and other techniques into a single discipline.

name: Yuliana
description: Psychotherapist at the University of Perm. Seduced by Oxana during her first year as an undergraduate.

name: Fantine
description: Tiny, fierce-looking woman of about sixty, dressed entirely in black. Lives in a first-floor apartment on the Quai Voltaire, Left Bank, Paris. Teaches Oxana Parisian style — fashion, hair, and scent — as a ruthlessly critical mentor. Sharp, dismissive, and imperious in manner.

name: Leoluca Messina
description: Lean, dark-haired man of about thirty-five with saturnine good looks. Sicilian, speaks Italian with the rasp of Sicily. Confident, commanding, and flirtatious with an edge of threat in his gaze. Wears an impeccable silver-grey shirt and a lightweight Brioni blazer.

name: Yiorgos Vlachos
description: Greek man purchasing radioactive cobalt-60 in Eastern Europe with probable intent to detonate a dirty bomb in Athens. Villanelle's first professional target.

name: Dragan Horvat
description: Balkan politician running a human trafficking network. Became infatuated with a seventeen-year-old heroin-addicted girl from Tbilisi and took her on expensive shopping sprees in European capital cities. Villanelle's second professional target.

name: Paolo Vella
description: Agent of the Polizia di Stato based in Palermo. Casual and warm in manner, with an informal Sicilian police register.

name: Anne-Laure Mercier
description: Villanelle's closest friend in Paris. Pretty, extroverted, and lonely. Former public relations professional, married to Gilles Mercier of the French Treasury. Sexually reckless. Lives on Avenue Kléber. Effusive, gossipy, and affectionate in manner, fond of endearments. Speaks in an upper-class Parisian social register.

name: Gilles Mercier
description: Senior functionary at the French Treasury. More than sixteen years older than his wife Anne-Laure. Passions include his wine cellar and ormolu clocks. Lives on Avenue Kléber, Paris.

name: Eve Polastri
description: MI5 analyst and head of the P3 section at Thames House. Twenty-nine years old, dark-haired with a make-up-free complexion and shoulder-length hair. Direct, analytical, and tenacious. Known among colleagues as a hunter who will not readily let go. Married to Niko Polastri.

name: Simon Mortimer
description: Eve's deputy at P3 in Thames House. In his twenties, with the arch solemnity of a metropolitan vicar. Formal, precise, and diplomatically tactful, with a civil service register.

name: Niko Polastri
description: Eve's husband, around 38-39 years old. Tall, with patient brown eyes and a cavalry officer moustache. Polish ex-chess champion who teaches maths, and a capable hacker. Bridge instructor at the West Hampstead club. Peppers his speech with Polish endearments.

name: Bill Tregaron
description: Eve's former head of section at P3. His career ended after the Horvat assassination occurred on his watch. Subsequently seconded to GCHQ in Cheltenham.

name: Nasreen Jilani
description: Pakistani writer who has received death threats. Scheduled to speak at the Oxford Union.

name: Reza Mokri
description: Iranian nuclear physicist requiring full protection during a UK visit.

name: Viktor Kedrin
description: Russian political theorist from St Petersburg State University. Broad, bearded, and charismatic. Advocates anti-American, pro-Eurasian superstate ideology. The ideologist and face of the movement.

name: Irema Beridze
description: Horvat's seventeen-year-old Georgian girlfriend. Heroin addict from Tbilisi.

name: Maria Golovkina
description: Russian female assassin used by the FSB for overseas hits. Former member of Russia's small-bore pistol squad at the Athens Olympics. Trained in covert assassination at the Spetsnaz base in Krasnodar. Found hanged in a hotel wardrobe in Brighton Beach.

name: Jelena Markovic
description: Serbian hitwoman attached to the notorious Zemun clan. Killed by a car bomb in Belgrade.

name: Aleksandr Simonov
description: Russian business oligarch suspected of funding Chechen and Dagestani militants. Shot outside the Frankfurt headquarters of the AltInvest Bank.

name: Mrs Shapiro
description: Blue-haired secretary of the West Hampstead Bridge Club.

name: Maciek
description: Niko Polastri's friend from Cracow University. They travelled across Europe in a battered van, competing in bridge, chess, and poker tournaments, retiring with over a million zloty between them.

name: Lucy Drake
description: Model and aspiring actress with vivid emerald green eyes and an oval, high-cheekboned face framed by a dark glossy bob. Lives in Queen's Park. Her striking resemblance to Villanelle is noted by Luis Martin on a London-bound flight. She is recruited by a man called Peter for a fake TV audition at the St Martin's Lane Hotel, unwittingly used as a decoy for the Kedrin operation: she checks into the Vernon Hotel as Julia Fanin, attends the Conway Hall lecture, then is flown to the Chateau Marmont in Los Angeles while the real killer takes her place. Arrested at Heathrow on a DNA match, she is interrogated at Paddington Green and released when the deception is exposed.

name: Luis Martín
description: Spanish modelling agency owner. Runs Tempest with his wife Elvira, with divisions in Paris, London, Milan and Moscow. Implausibly good-looking, flirtatious and narcissistic.

name: Sarah
description: Gallery owner in Mayfair. Late thirties, wheat-blonde hair in a chignon, wears a black cocktail dress. Meets Villanelle at a private view and invites her to dinner at Nobu. Lives in a flat above the gallery.

name: Roman Nikonov
description: Eighteen-year-old former pupil at Industrialny District secondary school in Perm. Sexually assaulted Anna Leonova while she waited for a late bus home. Arrested but released on a technicality after police botched the forensics. Castrated by Oxana in the woods by the Mulyanka river.

name: Gerald Watts
description: Reception manager at the Vernon Hotel on High Holborn. Has worked at the front desk for five years and considers himself an expert on accents. Checks in Villanelle under the Julia Fanin alias.

name: Vitaly Chuvarov
description: Political associate of Viktor Kedrin. Distinguished by his ponytail. Serves as master of ceremonies at Kedrin's Conway Hall lecture, introducing him to the audience in accented English.

name: DCI Gary Hurst
description: Detective Chief Inspector with the Metropolitan Police. Senior investigating officer at the Kedrin murder scene at the Vernon Hotel. Professional with dry humour and a direct manner. Briefs Eve on the four victims and the Julia Fanin alias.

name: Richard Edwards
description: Former MI6 station chief in Moscow, now head of the Russia desk. A very senior figure in the Intelligence world. Operates from a cover office at BQ Optics above Goodge Street. Formal, authoritative, and austere in manner. Recruits Eve for a classified solo mission to identify the Kedrin killer, revealing that elements within Thames House and Vauxhall Cross conspired to leave Kedrin unprotected.

name: Zhang Lei
description: Lieutenant Colonel in the People's Liberation Army and leader of the White Dragon crew, an elite cyber-warfare unit operating from Datong Road in Shanghai. A pudgy, bespectacled man who designed the ZeroT remote-access tool and has orchestrated cyber attacks against over 150 military and corporate targets.

name: Nurse Wu
description: Young woman working at a fetish establishment on Dangfeng Road in Pudong. Wears a crisp green and white nurse uniform with starched cap, surgical mask, and transparent plastic apron. Performs medical-themed BDSM sessions with clinical precision and a stern bedside manner.

name: Mikki Nakamura
description: Wife of a Japanese property developer in Shanghai. Stylish and gossipy. Patron of the Aquarium nightclub, which she introduced to Villanelle. Frequents the club on Friday nights, alone.

name: Janie Chou
description: Transgender woman in Shanghai. Willowy, with jasmine flowers in her hair. Speaks in broken, informal English. Works at the Aquarium nightclub for Alice Mao.

name: Howard
description: Young Malaysian man who owns a concept spa in Xintiandi, Shanghai. Part of the Aquarium nightclub social circle.

name: Alice Mao
description: Eurasian woman with pale glass-green eyes. Owner of the Aquarium nightclub and its building on the North Bund. One of the richest women in Shanghai, with a fortune from the sex trade. Proprietorial manner, cool regard, sharp intelligence.

name: Jin Qiang
description: MSS (Ministry of State Security) officer. Known to Richard Edwards from Moscow, where Jin was head of station. Quietly spoken figure in a dove-grey suit who speaks English with a faint American accent. At intervals a look of refined melancholy touches his features, with long manicured fingers. Maintains back-channel communications with British intelligence.

name: Ken Hung
description: Former Hong Kong University student who was completing an MA dissertation on the late poetry of Sylvia Plath when he was talent-spotted in a hotel steam room. Chiselled features and a gym-toned body. Known as the most famous porn star in China. Acquaintance of Alice Mao.

name: Calum Dennis
description: Station armourer at Dever Research Station in Hampshire. Responsible for firearms training and range supervision.

name: Kim
description: Franco-Vietnamese dancer at the Paris Opera Ballet, twenty-three years old. Lean and refined features reflecting his mixed heritage, with ivory-coloured skin and precisely defined musculature. Displays a lynx-like elegance.

name: Lance Pope
description: Career MI6 officer and veteran of numerous overseas postings. Experienced agent runner, commended by heads of station. Chronic online gambler, divorced, living alone in a one-room flat in Croydon. Fortyish, lean, with the suspicious features of a stoat. Dry, laconic, and sardonic in manner.

name: Billy Primrose
description: Former teenage hacker who used the online handle $qeeky, member of a hacker collective that attacked corporate and government websites. Recruited by MI6's Security Exploitation team after the group was taken down by FBI and Interpol. Asthmatic since childhood, with black-dyed hair, pale complexion, and a deathly back-bedroom pallor. Casual, irreverent in manner.

name: Lara Farmanyants
description: Former cadet at the Kazan Military Academy. Broad, high-cheekboned features, eyes the colour of slate, with severely cropped hair. A superb shot who competed in the pistol-shooting at the university games, winning bronze. Smart and ambitious, she left the military to work as an operative. Uses they/them pronouns and chose the name Charlie (Lara is their deadname). Self-described as gender non-binary. Admits to having feelings and being jealous of Eve's relationship with Oxana.

name: Tony Kent
description: Low-profile venture capitalist. Member of an exclusive fly-fishing syndicate on the River Itchen in Hampshire.

name: Dennis Cradle
description: Director of D4 Branch at MI5, responsible for counter-espionage against Russia and China. Tanned, bullish figure of about fifty. Lives in Muswell Hill.

name: Penny Cradle
description: Dennis Cradle's wife. Corporate lawyer. Prettyish, well-groomed, about five years younger than her husband.

name: Anton
description: Lean man with dark, cold eyes and the patchy beginnings of a beard. Speaks fluent Moscow-accented Russian and English. Identifies himself as a colleague of Konstantin within the Twelve. Commanding and pragmatic in manner.

name: Rinat Yevtukh
description: Head of Zoloty Bratstvo (Golden Brotherhood) mafia gang based in Odessa. Owns a fortified villa in Fontanka.

name: Zbigniew
description: Niko's old friend from Cracow University. Lectures at King's College London on Roman history, Augustus to Nero. Bear-like in build. Polish classics scholar with a dry wit.

name: Claudia
description: Zbig's girlfriend, an educational psychologist.

name: Gabriela Vukovic
description: Twenty-eight-year-old mistress of Dennis Cradle. Employed at the fitness club and spa at the Hotel du Littoral on the French Riviera.

name: Katya Goraya
description: Rinat Yevtukh's twenty-five-year-old lingerie model girlfriend. Demanding and materialistic.

name: Sergei (Cradle recruiter)
description: Young Russian, about thirty. Wears Brioni suits, speaks flawless English and perfectly accented French. Polished, cultured, and persuasive. Possesses an exceptional grasp of global strategy. Operates as a recruiter for the Twelve.

name: Rem Bakker
description: Poses as a Dutch IT consultant from Delft, with a wife named Gaite. Cover identity for a Twelve recruiter. Knowledgeable about wine and a Federer fan.

name: Marta
description: Housekeeper of Villanelle's building in Paris, living on the fifth floor. Keeps several kittens and their mother. Effusive and warm. Fond of the quiet young woman on the sixth floor, considering her a caring person.

name: Abby
description: Eve's contact at the Metropolitan Police Forensics Laboratory in Lambeth. A thorough forensic analyst with a professional yet friendly and informal manner.

name: Max Linder
description: Franco-Dutch far-right political activist and media celebrity. Twenty-nine years old, gay, with a platinum-blond quiff and orthodontically perfect teeth. Elegant in a high-buttoning Bavarian trachten jacket, he looks less like a politician than a member of a fascistically inclined boy band. His smile has something avid about it — a twist to the lips suggesting a hunger for the extreme. Has a huge following among young Europeans. His association with Nazism makes him a liability for the Twelve.

name: Mark
description: Eve and Niko's new neighbour. The youngest compliance manager his City bank has ever had. Wears strawberry-coloured trousers and a Guernsey sweater. Socially gauche and insensitive.

name: Leila
description: Zbigniew's girlfriend of several years. British-Jamaican heritage, grandparents born in Jamaica. Spirited, direct, and witty, with a practical streak.

name: Fiona
description: Mark's wife. Works in political public relations for the MP Gareth Wolf. Wears a covetable black Whistles coat. Mirthless smile, defensive and curt in manner.

name: Gareth Wolf
description: British MP for whom Fiona works in public relations. Known for persistent lying, rapacious self-interest, open contempt for the less fortunate, an expenses scandal involving his girlfriend's cosmetic surgery, and trade deals with Saudi Arabia.

name: Giovanna Bianchi
description: Director of the Van Diest boutique in Venice on Calle Vallaresso. Elegant woman of indeterminate age, direct and curious in manner. Expert in perfumes, with a mother who worked in a perfumery.

name: Bianca
description: Shop assistant at the Van Diest boutique in Venice. Speaks Russian and typically serves Russian-speaking clients.

name: Armando Trevisan
description: Questore (police chief) of Venice, based at the questura in Santa Croce. Lean figure in a dark suit, with economical movements and an attentive air. Half-English: his mother was English and he spent childhood summers in Tunbridge Wells watching Multi-Coloured Swap Shop. Speaks perfect English. Precise, formal yet cordial, and witty.

name: Umberto Zeni
description: Venetian jewellery designer. His new collection is launched at a reception at Palazzo Forlani.

name: Claudio Forlani
description: Son of the owner of Palazzo Forlani in Venice. Dark-haired, with aquiline features, a broken nose, amused brown eyes, and a sensual, deeply incised mouth. Wears crisp linen shirts and black velvet loafers embroidered with the Forlani family crest. Cultured, seductive, and ironic in manner.

name: Magali Le Meur
description: Leader of France's Nouvelle Droite party, advocate of pan-European nationalism. Broad, raw-boned features that look older in person than on the campaign posters plastered onto every derelict wall and motorway bridge in France. Beautiful eyes but a thin, intolerant mouth. Wears a thousand-euro Moncler coat and a Cartier diamond watch.

name: Todd Stanton
description: Former CIA psy-ops officer and expert in the harvesting and manipulation of online personal data. Often described as the dark cardinal of the American far right, widely believed to be the architect of the Republican Party's recent electoral victories. Corpulent frame, florid complexion, wearing a wolfskin coat.

name: Leonardo Venturi
description: Italian political theorist and founder of Lapsit Exillis, described on its website as an initiatory guild for aristocrats of the spirit. Tiny, wild-haired figure sporting a monocle. Explains the guild's mission in exhaustive detail.

name: Inka Järvi
description: Statuesque leader of Finland's Daughters of Odin.

name: Richard Baggot
description: Leader of the UK Patriots Party. Paunchy figure with a crocodile grin, wearing a loud tweed suit at the Felsnadel gathering.

name: Silas Orr-Hadow
description: Upper-caste Tory whose family has furnished England with several generations of fascist sympathisers. Pencil-thin. Wears lederhosen and white knee socks at the Felsnadel.

name: Johanna
description: Temporary worker from a local Innsbruck personnel agency at the Felsnadel. Close-set eyes, freckles, and a substantial bust zipped into a pink quilted jacket. Chatty and gossipy, reminds Villanelle of Khriusha the Pig from a Russian children's TV series.

name: Klaus Lorenz
description: Bassist of Panzerdämmerung, one of the two Lorenz brothers. Sharply handsome, outfitted in a black uniform with a distinctly military edge.

name: Peter Lorenz
description: Drummer of Panzerdämmerung, brother of Klaus. Sharply handsome, outfitted in a black uniform with a military edge.

name: Petra Voss
description: Lead singer and guitarist of Panzerdämmerung. Imperious and pantherine, with a severe bob of dark hair. Performs in a white blouse, calf-length skirt, and boots, carrying a blood-red Fender Stratocaster guitar slung like an assault rifle. Her singing voice can shift from soft and picking to steely and insinuating. During the Felsnadel torch ceremony she stares straight at Villanelle, expressionless.

name: Birgit
description: Manager employed by Max Linder at the Felsnadel. Severely dressed woman in her forties. Enforces fetishistically precise rules on hairstyles, make-up, and perfume. Inspects the staff's fingernails, teeth, and hygiene. Authoritarian, brusque, and controlling, with a formal register. Maria describes her as a fucking Nazi, basically, like Linder. Rumoured to have whipped a girl once for wearing mascara.

name: Maria
description: Villanelle's Polish roommate at the Felsnadel. Pale and young, wearing a tracksuit and woollen beanie. Tired and resigned but supportive. Warns Villanelle that Johanna is a spy for Birgit and recites the house rules.

name: Sim Henderson
description: Russian-speaking investigator in the Economic Crimes department of the City of London Police. Explains the structure of Russian private military companies (ChVKs) and the deniable operations arm SF12 to Billy Primrose.

name: Yuri Modin
description: Historical figure. Former KGB handler of the Cambridge spies (Philby, Burgess, Maclean, Blunt). Guest lecturer at the Andropov Academy, where he taught the Active Measures course covering subversion, disinformation, and assassination. Konstantin Orlov was his protégé. Wrote a book about the Cambridge spies. Richard Edwards met him at a party in Moscow.

name: Oleg
description: Russian contact sent by Richard Edwards to arrange logistics for Eve's Moscow trip. In his late thirties, forty at a push, with skin that hasn't seen the sun in months and an expressionless voice. Handles visas and travel arrangements with brisk efficiency. Speaks basic English with a direct, informal manner. Meets Eve on the platform at Goodge Street station.

name: Vadim Tikhomirov
description: FSB general who became the sitting Russian president. Broad-shouldered, silver-haired, mid-fifties with a buzz-cut and a wry urbane smile. His regime is widely viewed as bullying and corrupt. Faces a major threat from opposition candidate Anna Volkonskaya. Previously helped Eve escape from Russia after the VDNKh Metro station incident and discreetly alerted her that Richard Edwards was an asset of the Twelve.

name: Amanda Edwards
description: Wife of Richard Edwards. Artist working in watercolours and drawings. Exhibits at a gallery in Chiswick overlooking the Thames.

name: Chloe Edwards
description: Daughter of Richard and Amanda Edwards. Long-lashed eyes and her mother's bone structure. Aspiring actress auditioning for drama school in the New Year. Exuberant and friendly, with direct colloquial language. Gives Eve a card with an embossed skull and crossbones. As a teenager, became infatuated with a charming older Russian who visited their house in Saint-Remy-de-Provence.

name: Colin Dye
description: Shop assistant at Whitlock and Jones medical supplies on Welbeck Street, London. Fond of making puns on his surname. Informal in manner.

name: Igor
description: Russian deckhand on the container ship Kirovo-Chepetsk. Has an aunt named Galya who drives a tram in Smolensk. Agrees to smuggle Eve and Villanelle from Immingham to St Petersburg in exchange for the Ducati motorcycle and a photograph of Eve's breasts.

name: Dasha Kvariani
description: Georgian-Russian woman with cool green eyes and lustrous chestnut hair cut in a chin-length bob. Known as Necksnapper from the Dobryanka penal colony, where she was an inmate alongside Villanelle. Wears a black shearling jacket. Her father was a respected gang leader in the vorovskoy mir of St Petersburg. Convicted for breaking a man's neck on the St Petersburg Metro after he groped her; tried in Perm, 1500 kilometres from home. Direct and menacing, she works as head of security for the owner of the Prekrasnaya Nevesta warehouse. Speaks fluent Russian and rudimentary English with intentional grammatical errors.

name: Gennadi Kvariani
description: Father of Dasha Kvariani. Former leader of a Kupchino Bratva brigade that controlled the heroin trade in St Petersburg. So powerful that local prosecutors did not dare try Dasha in a local court, sending her 1500 kilometres to Perm instead. Retired, passing the brigade leadership to his daughter.

name: Kristina
description: Young woman living in Dasha Kvariani's apartment in Avtovo, Dasha's partner. Tall and long-legged, with hair the colour of wheat and a gentle, hesitant manner. Known as Kris. An unlikely member of a gangster household. Rarely speaks, and when she does it is so quietly that others strain to hear her. Kind and perceptive, with a rare shy smile. Possesses an uncanny ability to recognise professional killers on sight.

name: Asmat Dzabrati
description: Pakhan (supreme boss) of the Kupchino Bratva in St Petersburg. Short, with thinning hair and mild, rabbity eyes. The least remarkable-looking man in the room, yet immediately the centre of attention. Wields the kind of power that doesn't proclaim itself but is evident in the demeanour of others. A gangster of the old school, known in his younger days for dispatching rivals with an axe. Dasha's boss, to whom the four brigadiers report.

name: Angelina
description: Wife of Pavel, a boyevik in Dasha Kvariani's brigade. Nervous eyes and hair the colour of burnt caramel. Pragmatic and materialistic, decorates her décolletage with tiny gold stars. Conversations revolve around clothes, children, and unfaithful husbands.

name: Pavel
description: Husband of Angelina. Boyevik (soldier) in Dasha Kvariani's brigade of the Kupchino Bratva. Says he doesn't mind working for a woman because she's clever like a man.

name: Yelena
description: Ex-wife of Pakhan Asmat Dzabrati. Lives in one of his apartments in the building on Malaya Balkanskaya Ulitsa, near Kupchino metro station. Disapproves of the Pakhan's relationship with his young mistress Zoya.

name: Rushana
description: Sister of Pakhan Asmat Dzabrati. Lives with her husband in the building on Malaya Balkanskaya Ulitsa. Acts as hostess when the Pakhan receives bratva members and their families.

name: Zoya
description: Twenty-four-year-old Ukrainian woman, the Pakhan Asmat Dzabrati's sugar baby. Met him through an introduction agency. Lives in a small apartment behind the Fruzensky department store in St Petersburg, leased by the Pakhan. Gets Botox shots at a clinic on Nevsky Prospekt. The Pakhan's family and ex-wife refuse to acknowledge her.

name: Nadezhda Vorontsova
description: Mother of Villanelle/Oxana. Raised on a farm near Novozybkov; her family was originally from Chuvashia. Very beautiful in the Chuvash manner, with a high forehead, long dark hair, and an expression of surprise given by the arch of her eyebrows. At fifteen, the Chernobyl disaster contaminated her village (150km away); she was evacuated and the area became a Closed Zone. She moved to Perm, married Boris Vorontsov at twenty-two. Suffered from an illness, probably radiation-related, spending entire days in bed. Took young Oxana to the Kungur ice caves as a special outing. Died a few weeks later.

name: Dima
description: Assistant to FSB General Vadim Tikhomirov. Handsome young man in a winter camouflage uniform. Present at the armoured truck during Tikhomirov's secret roadside meeting with Eve.

name: Nobby
description: Former sniper section leader in E Squadron (MI6 black operations). Now an instructor and watchdog at Knock Tom. Trains Villanelle/Oxana and Anton. Tells anecdotes about women from the clubs on Brentwood High Street. Prepares meals in the platform canteen — warm baked beans and luncheon meat. Informal and comradely in manner, with barrack-room humour.

name: Ginge
description: Former sniper section leader in E Squadron. Stocky, balding Welshman with a twinkling, mischievous smile. Instructor at Knock Tom for Charlie and Eve. Expert in ballistics — range, wind, drag, spin-drift, and the Coriolis effect. Calls Charlie Charlie-girl until corrected. Compares teams with Nobby: concedes Oxana may be more of a natural marksman but backs Charlie long-term. Pragmatic and cheerful, mixing Welsh-accented military slang with good-natured banter.

name: Valery Stechkin
description: President of the Russian Federation. Presides like a latter-day Roman emperor over a sclerotic and corrupt state. Possesses kompromat on Ronald Loy that has allowed him to steer the White House. Rouged, powdered appearance.

name: Ronald Loy
description: President of the United States of America. Subject of kompromat held by Stechkin: video of him watching sex workers in a hotel suite. Tiny, childlike hands. Disdainful expression. Rouged, powdered appearance.

name: Inna Lapotnikova
description: Customs officer at Sheremetyevo Airport. Older woman with tiny, granite eyes. Expressionless and professional. Silently takes Eve's concealed message from her passport and leaves the room to presumably forward it to Tikhomirov.

name: Tolya
description: Paramilitary operative of the Twelve. Hard-eyed young man carrying a sub-machine gun slung over his leather jacket. Brings up the rear as armed escort during the underground tunnel journey.

name: Gennadi
description: Former KGB agent, one of the last kroty (moles) who knows the underground tunnel network beneath Moscow. Older man in a crumpled coat with a miner's helmet headlamp. Has seen Stalinist-era skeletons in the tunnels — identifiable by the bullet holes in the back of their skulls. Dry and sardonic: Jesus never came down here.

name: Alexei
description: Plainclothes FSB officer who escorts Eve on the two-day drive from Moscow to Perm. Divorced, with a girlfriend named Vika (librarian at MSU) and a dog named Archie. Classical music enthusiast, especially Rachmaninoff. Veteran of the Dubrovka Theatre siege.

name: Galya
description: Fellow linguistics student of Oxana at St Petersburg University. Hair-trigger temperament, forever taking offence.

name: Olga
description: Fellow linguistics student of Oxana at St Petersburg University. Known for her smug veganism.

name: Andris
description: Fellow linguistics student of Oxana at St Petersburg University. Characterized by insufferable virtue-signalling.

name: Nastya
description: Linguistics lecturer at St Petersburg University. Young, attractive academic, married to a city duma deputy. Oxana flirts with her in class.

name: Max
description: MI6 agent who posed as a Twelve operative at Apokalipsis. Lean, dark, thirty-something. Speaks flawless Russian but not a native speaker. Direct and self-assured in a linen suit with Gucci tie. At Transept House he admits lying to Villanelle and describes Eve as an SIS officer. Killed by Villanelle with a ceramic blade to the jugular — he bleeds out against a Gestetner photocopier.

name: Balice St Clair
description: MI6 officer on the Russia desk who uses the alias Maria. Almost translucently pale skin, intense and magnetic presence. Posed as a Twelve operative at Apokalipsis to recruit Villanelle for a false-flag assassination. Flirtatious, strokes V s bicep at Harrods lunch. V grabs her by the throat to assert dominance. Arranged the Harrods meeting at the Guerlain counter.

name: Ron Tiberius
description: American billionaire, massively but mysteriously wealthy. Residences in New York, London, Cap Ferrat, Telluride, and St Barth s. Personal friend of heads of state, media proprietors, billionaire entrepreneurs. In his fifties. Deals in favours and influence. Predatory paedophile who likes them young — uses trafficked teenagers and blackmails powerful guests with compromising photographs.

name: Myrtha Willis
description: Former media trainee for the British Conservative Party. Had an affair with Tiberius. Now serves as his official date when needed and his primary scout for young girls, using his film and TV production companies as bait.

name: Lev Leonov
description: Young Russian oligarch, owner of the Apokalipsis club on the Griboyedov Canal in St Petersburg. Very much on the make.

name: Adam Fentiman
description: British politician, likely future Foreign Secretary. Oxford contemporary of the incoming Prime Minister, possible former fling. Former army officer with a shrapnel wound from the Gulf War. Compromised by a photograph with a minor on Tiberius yacht at La Napoule. Now essentially Tiberius puppet.

name: Sergei Stepanenko
description: Member of the Twelve. Fortyish, steel-grey hair, round spectacles, vaguely academic air. Quiet and cultivated, uses historical art references as conversational cover. Meets Villanelle at the State Russian Museum to reveal that Max and Maria are not Twelve operatives.

name: Kira
description: Young woman in a tailored grey suit who greets Villanelle at a building near Moscow Station in St Petersburg. Formal in manner. Appears to be a Twelve operative or associate.

name: Darren
description: Young, nervous, narrow-shouldered MI6 officer who manages the safe house on Ventnor Road in North Finchley. Apologises constantly, offers instant coffee and soya milk, asks for expense receipts. Suggests using the shower rather than the bath to save costs. The previous occupant was a drug gang boss with dandruff.

name: Pippa
description: Tour leader of the Ossa and Pelion horticultural tour. About fifty, with choppy blonde hair, silk scarf knotted around her neck. Prepared to keep up appearances come what may. Shepherds the group with patient efficiency.

name: Johnny Fernandes
description: Spry, silver-haired man of indeterminate age. Cover identity: retired Anglo-Asian widower with a passion for rose-growing. No stranger to the profession of arms. Iron grip and trim military bearing.

name: Ingo Vance
description: Theatre actor on the Ossa and Pelion horticultural tour. Theatrical personality, tells stories of blanking on stage during Hamlet or one of the Henrys. Married to Teazel Vance.

name: Teazel Vance
description: Former teenage star of the 1970s TV series The Gumdrops, where she played Milly Mauve the lead singer. Pixie features, sits bolt upright, narrow shoulders wrapped in pashmina shawls. Reminds Villanelle of a mummified cat in the Hermitage Museum. Married to Ingo Vance.

name: William Mortishead
description: Distinguished if dishevelled historian, writing a book about the younger Pitt. Mild blue gaze. Member of the Ossa and Pelion horticultural tour. Married to Lorna Mortishead.

name: Lorna Mortishead
description: Sixtyish, with glossy chestnut hair and the bulging eyes of a spaniel. Semi-retired freelance editor. Member of the Ossa and Pelion horticultural tour. Married to William Mortishead.

name: Dan Melbury
description: TV celebrity who presents Topsoil on Channel 4. Expert garden lecturer for the Ossa and Pelion horticultural tour. His arrival sends a stir through the St Pancras departures hall.

name: Lorelei Vane-Muir
description: Dramatic and outspoken member of the Ossa and Pelion horticultural tour. Arrives in a state of some confusion, loudly greets William Mortishead as darling, orders gin-tonic at 7am on the Eurostar. Calls the others dull dogs.

name: Sylvie
description: Pornographic actress from Nice, twenty-four years old but looks much younger, like sixteen. Specialises in barely legal roles. Recruited by MI6 through coercion over unpaid taxes on her earnings. Approached by Myrtha Willis at a cafe in St-Sepulcre and invited to Ron Tiberius villa on Saturday.

name: Comtesse de Quincy
description: Owner of the Pavillon du Rossignol near Poisy in Provence. Slender, silver-haired, wearing worn dungarees and tennis shoes. Her garden is a memorial to her late husband.

name: Anna Volkonskaya
description: Opposition candidate in the Russian presidential election. Nationalist-traditionalist platform backed by the Orthodox Church. Champion of old values: duty, honour, and religious observance. A direct threat to President Tikhomirov. Possibly aligned with the Twelve.

name: Valentin Dominik
description: Twenty-six-year-old adopted son of Nikolai Dominik, lives in the family mansion at Kensington Palace Gardens. Got a first from the LSE but is very much a party boy. Features in Tatler and Vogue. Has haemophilia B (not a weak heart as Nikolai claims). A direct descendant of the Romanovs: his great-grandmother was Alexandra Nikolaevna, secret daughter of Tsar Nicholas II, smuggled to France by Philippe de Lyon in 1903. Grew up poor in Marseilles, found by Sergei at age twelve. Dreams of painting birds. At the centre of Operation Sanctify.

name: Nikolai Dominik
description: Wealthy Russian emigre patriarch. Old money — grandfather Feliks was a White Guard general who fled Russia in 1917 with gold and jewels. Family has fingers in art, property, banking. Despises Tikhomirov as a jumped-up functionary. Supports nationalist-traditionalist causes and the Orthodox Church.

name: Uttara Khedkar
description: Daughter of a Bollywood film producer. Student at a culinary school in South Kensington. Stopped outside a Park Lane nightclub with two dozen wraps of cocaine. Blackmailed by Balice into serving as a waitress-spy at the Indian Military and Sporting Club in exchange for avoiding prison.

name: Anastasia Dominik
description: Wife of Nikolai Dominik and mother of Valentin. Volumised honey-blonde hair, cosmetically smoothed skin, and sulky indulged features of a Persian cat. Stoic and magnificent in crises: manages Valentin drunk collapse with composure, makes sandwiches, calls Armani Couture for Eve replacement jacket.

name: Charlie St Clair
description: Husband of Balice St Clair. Fast-track MI5 officer, now head of technical and surveillance operations including the elite A4 watchers team. Inter-service marriage with Balice, one of convenience for both. Not a first-class brain but makes her laugh. Fond of lingerie. Probably having an affair with his PA Dawn.

name: Dawn
description: Personal assistant to Charlie St Clair at MI5. Probably having an affair with Charlie. Has a knowing glint in her eye when she meets Balice.

name: Bilyana Ilieva
description: Bulgarian woman from Targovishte. Recruited by the Twelve at the Home Office during her British citizenship application — told she was working for British Intelligence. Handler was Johnny Fernandes. Sent to spy on and entrap Balice St Clair via a gym honey trap. Confesses the truth to Balice and proposes a deal: marriage for citizenship and a genuine MI6 job. Strong, direct, cheetah-like stillness. Wide-set gaze.

name: Cecil Phipps
description: Deputy to Balice St Clair on the MI6 Russia desk. Described as obnoxious and oleaginous. Sent to the Cabinet Office meeting at Downing Street in Balice place.

name: Dimitri
description: Russian security guard at the Dominik Mansion. Works alongside Pavel. Will follow Valentin and the women at a distance to Nate Paget party.

name: Nate Paget
description: Co-proprietor of Caspian's casino in Berkeley Square alongside Crispin Vale. Former school friend of Valentin Dominik. Tall, slender, with shadowy ironic eyes. Condescending toward those he considers beneath him.

name: Larissa
description: Maid at the Dominik Mansion. Brings trays of coffee to the guest rooms.

name: Philippe de Lyon
description: Mysterious French historical figure. Arrived in St Petersburg in 1901, destitute, after the birth of Nicholas II fourth daughter Anastasia. Promised Tsarina Alexandra a male heir — she miscarried and Philippe returned to France exceedingly wealthy. Alexei was not born until 1904. His portrait hangs in both the Dominik Mansion and a major Russian art gallery. Sergei pointed him out to Villanelle.

name: Denis Borzhko
description: Senior GRU officer. Dark-haired with a rough beard.

name: Gyorgiy Peskov
description: Senior GRU officer. Blond and heavy-set. Storms a Mayfair club with Borzhko armed with automatic pistols. Shot by Eve through the thumb and forefinger of his gun hand, then beaten and restrained by four dwarfs. Hospitalised at St Mary's under police guard.

name: Pavel (Dominik security)
description: Russian security man employed by the Dominik family at Kensington Palace Gardens. Works alongside Dimitri. Suggests following Valentin at a distance to the Mayfair club. Takes breakfast up to Valentin after the attack.

name: Alexandra Nikolaevna
description: Secret daughter of Tsar Nicholas II and Tsarina Alexandra of Russia, born in 1903. Officially the pregnancy was declared a miscarriage. Philippe de Lyon smuggled her out of Russia as a newborn. She grew up in Avignon with a childless couple. Married, had a daughter, and that daughter had Valentin. Biological great-grandmother of Valentin Dominik.

name: Indrani Sen
description: Senior Intensive Care physician at St Marys Hospital in Paddington. Professional and efficient, with brisk economical movements. Treats Valentin Dominik for intracranial haemorrhage caused by haemophilia.

name: Irene Mendoza
description: Filipino staff nurse at St Marys Hospital. Professional and caring, uses the nursing we. Moves Valentin from ICU to a side room. Wears Crocs.

name: Vyacheslav Golovkin
description: Press secretary to Anna Volkonskaya, the Russian presidential candidate. Formal and controlled, speaks in a terse register. Pressures Balice to locate Valentin Dominik.

name: Dido
description: Niko Polastri's new partner. Red-haired, works in public relations. Has a golden retriever named Haribo. Nervous and easily embarrassed, polite and friendly.

name: Majid Ismailov
description: Patriarch of the Ismailov family. Iron-grey hair, hawkish features, sun-creased eyes. Metals magnate and owner of Buxor mining company. Billionaire, sixty-five years old. The most powerful man in Naziristan — the power behind the presidential throne. Responsible for scrapping capital controls and privatising state property. Passionate about horse racing — owns over twenty thoroughbreds at Newmarket. Played polo at Cambridge. Dreams of winning the Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe with Beulah Boy. Former wife Gulnara died of leukaemia. The Foreign Office considers him our bad boy.

name: Shirin Ismailova
description: Majid Ismailov daughter. Manages the racing operation and is named heir to Buxor and all Ismailov interests. Knowledgeable about horses, warm, protective of Budoor.

name: Farad Ismailov
description: Son of Majid Ismailov. Educated at Trinity College, Oxford (PPE), and the Royal Military Academy, Sandhurst. Destined to succeed his father as leader of Naziristan, though he dreads the role. Compulsive gambler — debt at Caspian's has escalated to nearly half a million pounds after a catastrophic night using the Orphelins system. Desperately obsessed with Mimi Stackridge. Feels like an outsider in British high society despite his wealth. Drives an ice-blue Bentley. Controlled and polite but with repressed anger.

name: Camilla Best
description: Model and face of Dior. Seen at Caspian's casino swapping secrets with Nate Paget and Crispin Vale.

name: Crispin Vale
description: Co-proprietor of Caspian's casino in Berkeley Square alongside Nate Paget.

name: Will Brookes
description: Former Camden Market stallholder who launched Rhino, a cryptocurrency. Farad lost a few hundred thousand pounds investing in it.

name: Mimi Stackridge
description: Pale, thin woman of indeterminate age. Wears an ivory satin dress. Speaks with a degraded private-school English accent. Uses cocaine. Flat, detached, and brutally direct in manner.

name: Gulnara Ismailova
description: Former wife of Majid Ismailov, mother of Farad and Shirin. Divorced Majid a decade ago. Lived in Pakhtun, the Naziristani capital, preferring it to London. Died of leukaemia.

name: Adam Shaw
description: MI6 officer sent by Balice to brief V and Eve on the Ismailov mission. Hair combed back, subtly superior manner. Raised in the country, well acquainted with horses and racing. Professional and slightly condescending.

name: Josh Riordan
description: Racehorse trainer based near Newmarket. Trains most of Majid Ismailov's twenty-odd horses.

name: Davy McPhee
description: Stable lad who works with Budoor and Sofinisba at Josh Riordan's training yard near Newmarket.

name: Joe
description: Lead jockey at Josh Riordans yard. Will ride Beulah Boy in the Arc de Triomphe. Wiry, cheerful-looking, wearing a riding hat and raincoat. Laconic, understated, and perceptive. Irish — remarks that in Ireland they have horses instead of psychiatrists.

name: Oliver Reynolds
description: School friend of Farad who inherited his fathers training yard near Bury St Edmunds. His father Rory Reynolds was a famous jump jockey who won the Grand National on The Bizzler before becoming a successful trainer. Rory died suddenly a couple of years earlier of a heart condition. Ollie is a heavy drinker, and the yard has probably declined since his fathers time.

name: Sir Gavin Rycart
description: Commissioner of the Metropolitan Police of London. Speaks to Balice about the Marcus Hunt case and insists the Met cannot afford another scandal.

name: Marcus Hunt
description: Undercover police officer found battered to death in Regent Park. Had a significant history of alleged sexual offences that evaded vetting. His death prompted concerns about scandal within the Metropolitan Police.

name: Wayne
description: Corrupt police officer and colleague of Marcus Hunt. Ginger-haired with a round head. Carries a taser and a Glock. Vulgar, sexist, and physically intimidating. Killed by Villanelle in the M11 motel.

name: Azim
description: Young Naziristani man in the service of Majid Ismailov at Great Ditton. Works in the kitchen alongside Zafar, preparing traditional Naziristani dishes.

name: Zafar
description: Young Naziristani man in the service of Majid Ismailov at Great Ditton. Works in the kitchen alongside Azim, preparing traditional Naziristani dishes.

name: Viktor Batyrshin
description: Pakhtun man captured at the Santon Stream bridge ransom exchange. Connected to the Budoor kidnapping operation.

name: Rustam Dzhuraev
description: Naziristani mastermind behind the Budoor kidnapping. Educated, fluent in Russian and English. Frequented the Cafe Bulbul in Pakhtun where he recruited Viktor Batyrshin. Meticulously planned the entire operation: theft, logistics, ransom, and promised Viktor a British visa and wife. Childhood friend of Farad Ismailov.

name: Pete
description: Farrier who works at Josh Riordans yard. Large and burly, with calloused grimy hands and a leather apron. His girlfriend Errin works at Oliver Reynolds yard. Recognised Budoor disguised as Frisky Lou at Reynolds stable.

name: Errin
description: Pete's girlfriend, working as a stable lad at Oliver Reynolds's yard.

name: Captain Gladstone
description: Captain at the Dever Research Station MI6 safe house in Hampshire. Dry sense of humour, chess player, offers wine and Babys Leg pudding.

name: Philippe Devereaux
description: French racehorse owner. Owns Prince d'Aquitaine, the favourite for the Arc de Triomphe, based at Chantilly. In his late forties, wry and saturnine good looks. Wears Eau Sauvage. Extremely self-assured and lizardy — Eve imagines a forked tongue catching a fly.

name: Shiki Goto
description: Japanese racehorse owner. Owns Kintarou, a horse running in the Arc de Triomphe. Scrupulously polite. An enthusiast of Sailor Moon, comparing Eve's relationship to Sailor Uranus and Sailor Neptune.

name: Mirzoev
description: Naziristani ambassador to France. Scrupulously polite at the formal lunch. His eyes narrow disapprovingly when Eve mentions leaving her husband for a woman.
